q: Biology is broadly divided into five major themes: organization, information, energy and matter, interactions and evolution.
The hierarchy of life on earth is unfolded from atom to biosphere.
With each set up, new properties emerge (emergent properties) because of interactions among components at the lower levels.
A correlation of structure and function can be observed at each level of the biological hierarchy.
The hummingbird's anatomy allows the wings to rotate at the shoulder, which is a unique ability among birds to fly backward or hover in place.
The cell is the lowest level of organization that can perform all activities required for life.
Eukaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles, including DNA in nucleus.
Prokaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles.
The genetic information is encoded in the nucleotide sequences of DNA.
DNA transmits heritable information from parents to offspring.
DNA sequences (called genes) act as instructions to make a cell's protein production by being transcribed into mRNA and then translated into specific proteins.
Gene expression refers solely to the process of translating mRNA into proteins.
Gene expression can produce some RNAs that are not translated into proteins but serve other important functions.
All organisms require energy for their life.
Producers convert energy from sunlight to chemical energy and some of which is then passed to consumers.
Energy flows through the ecosystem, usually entering as light and exiting as heat.
Chemical cycle occurs between organisms and the environment.
In feedback regulation, a process is regulated by its output or product.
In negative feedback, accumulation of the product slows its production.
In positive feedback, a product speeds up its own production.
Feedback is a type of regulation common to life at all levels, from molecules to ecosystems.
Evolution is the process of change that has transformed life on Earth and accounts for the unity and diversity of life.
Domain Bacteria and Archaea consist of prokaryotes.
Domain Eukarya includes various groups of protists as well as fungi, plants, and animals.
Bacteria are the most diverse and widespread prokaryotes and are now classified into multiple kingdoms.
Protists are mostly unicellular eukaryotes.
Kingdom Plantae consists of multicellular eukaryotes that carry out photosynthesis, the conversion of light energy to the chemical energy in food.
Kingdom Fungi is defined in part by the nutritional mode of its members, which absorb nutrients from outside their bodies.
Kingdom Animalia consists of multicellular eukaryotes that ingest other organisms.
Darwin (1809-1882) proposed natural selection as the mechanism for evolutionary adaptation of populations to their environments.

Natural selection is defined as the evolutionary process that occurs when a population is exposed to environmental factors that consistently cause individuals with certain heritable traits to have greater reproductive success than do individuals with other heritable traits.
